summary: "Top right corner" is materially ambiguous: the app has no viewport-spanning top bar today, the toggle currently lives in the left sidebar's top row, and per-page headers already reserve their top-right slot for a primary action. Four requester-only decisions remain (what "top right" means structurally, which layouts change, whether a new bar hosts anything else, and how to avoid competing with per-page primary actions).

findings.issue: Meaning of "top right corner" is undefined given there is no app-wide top bar: could be a new top bar over the main content, the sidebar's top-right, or each page's header row. This changes the scope of the work significantly.
Scope across layouts is unstated — the toggle also appears on the sign-in/auth screen (no sidebar); unclear whether that placement should change too.
If a new top bar is introduced, it is unclear whether it should contain only the toggle or also other controls (breadcrumbs, page title, Sign out).
On pages with an existing top-right primary action, the requester must confirm whether a shared top bar above page headers is acceptable to avoid the toggle competing with that action.
